edges.description: Reduced repolarization reserve prolongs the ventricular action potential and creates an arrhythmogenic substrate.
Persistent inward sodium current prolongs ventricular repolarization and feeds the common arrhythmogenic substrate.
edges.source: Persistent Late Sodium Current in LQT3

edges.description: LQTS3 sodium-channel dysfunction can affect sinoatrial-node pacemaker activity and present with sinus bradycardia.
edges.source: Persistent Late Sodium Current in LQT3

edges.description: Prolonged ventricular action potential duration permits early afterdepolarizations to emerge from the plateau.
Delayed ventricular repolarization is recorded clinically as QT-interval prolongation on basal electrocardiography.
Abnormal ventricular repolarization can produce abnormal T-wave morphology on the electrocardiogram.
Early afterdepolarization-triggered ventricular tachyarrhythmias produce syncope, cardiac arrest, and sudden death.
Genotype-specific triggering circumstances precipitate malignant ventricular arrhythmias against the repolarization substrate.
Torsades de pointes commonly presents as transient loss of consciousness from arrhythmic cerebral hypoperfusion.
Arrhythmic loss-of-consciousness events in LQTS can present clinically as seizure or seizure-like episodes before the cardiac diagnosis is recognized.
Sustained torsades de pointes or degeneration to ventricular fibrillation can progress to cardiac arrest.
Untreated torsades de pointes can degenerate to a fatal ventricular arrhythmia.
Sustained torsades de pointes abolishes effective cardiac output, causing arrhythmic syncope and, when the rhythm does not terminate, sudden cardiac death.

nodes.description: Abnormal T-wave morphology on ECG is a frequent electrocardiographic feature of familial LQTS and contributes to clinical recognition.

evidence.reference_title: The inherited long QT syndrome: from ion channel to bedside.
evidence.snippet: The signs of long QT syndrome are prolongation of the QT interval on the electrocardiogram and abnormalities of T wave morphology.

evidence.reference_title: Romano-Ward syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0005135 | Abnormal T-wave | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: Life-threatening events in familial LQTS are shaped by genotype-specific triggering circumstances. The classical LQT1-LQT3 subtypes differ in whether episodes cluster during exercise or rest/sleep, which informs risk stratification and anti-adrenergic management.

evidence.reference_title: Genotype-phenotype correlation in the long-QT syndrome: gene-specific triggers for life-threatening arrhythmias.
evidence.snippet: LQT1 patients experienced the majority of their events (62%) during exercise, and only 3% occurred during rest/sleep. These percentages were almost reversed among LQT2 and LQT3 patients, who were less likely to have events during exercise (13%) and more likely to have events during rest/sleep (29% and 39%).

nodes.description: Emerging precision therapeutic strategy targeting loss-of-function KCNH2 variants in LQT2 patients. RNA interference selectively suppresses mutant KCNH2 transcript while preserving wild-type expression, alleviating dominant-negative effects and restoring hERG current density.
First-line anti-adrenergic therapy for most patients with inherited long QT syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Congenital long QT syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Treatment should always begin with beta-blockers, unless there are valid contraindications.

evidence.reference_title: Effectiveness and limitations of beta-blocker therapy in congenital long-QT syndrome.
evidence.snippet: beta-blockers are associated with a significant reduction in cardiac events in LQTS patients. However, syncope, aborted cardiac arrest, and LQTS-related death continue to occur while patients are on prescribed beta-blockers

nodes.description: Sustained torsades de pointes or degeneration to ventricular fibrillation can cause resuscitated or fatal cardiac arrest.

evidence.reference_title: Congenital long QT syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The two cardinal manifestations of LQTS are syncopal episodes, that may lead to cardiac arrest and sudden cardiac death, and electrocardiographic abnormalities, including prolongation of the QT interval and T wave abnormalities.

nodes.description: Regulatory in-silico proarrhythmia model built on the O'Hara-Rudy ORd model by replacing the I_Kr formulation with a Markov model of dynamic drug-hERG (I_Kr) binding. Combined with multi-channel pharmacology (up to seven currents), it computes the net-charge metric qNet to stratify drug-induced torsades de pointes (TdP) risk. This is the acquired (drug-induced) long QT / proarrhythmia arm and is the consensus cell model of the FDA/HESI CiPA initiative.

evidence.reference_title: Optimization of an In silico Cardiac Cell Model for Proarrhythmia Risk Assessment.
evidence.snippet: we modified the original O'Hara Rudy ventricular cell mathematical model to include a Markov model of IKr to represent dynamic drug-IKr interactions (IKr-dynamic ORd model)

nodes.description: Prolonged ventricular action potential duration promotes plateau oscillations and early afterdepolarizations that trigger torsades de pointes.

computational_models.description: I_Kr block during slow pacing induces early afterdepolarizations in the model, reproducing the arrhythmogenic trigger.
computational_models.name: O'Hara-Rudy (ORd) Human Ventricular Action Potential Model

evidence.reference_title: Congenital Long QT syndrome and torsade de pointes.

evidence.reference_title: The inherited long QT syndrome: from ion channel to bedside.
evidence.snippet: The delayed recovery predisposes individuals to the development of early afterdepolarizations and initiation of torsade de pointes arrhythmias.

nodes.description: Surgical anti-adrenergic escalation when recurrent syncope or major events persist despite full-dose beta-blockade.

evidence.reference_title: Congenital long QT syndrome.
evidence.snippet: If the patient has one more syncope despite a full dose beta-blockade, left cardiac sympathetic denervation (LCSD) should be performed without hesitation

nodes.description: Precision pharmacotherapy most relevant to the SCN5A/LQT3 subtype, where late sodium current blockade shortens QTc and lowers arrhythmic event burden.

Foundational system of ordinary differential equations for the undiseased human ventricular cardiomyocyte action potential, fit to human myocyte data for the major ionic currents (I_CaL, I_Kr, I_Ks, I_K1, I_to, late I_Na, I_NaCa, I_NaK) with intracellular Ca2+ and CaMKII handling. Long QT syndrome is simulated by reducing repolarizing K+ conductance (I_Kr for LQT2, I_Ks for LQT1) or increasing the persistent late Na+ current (LQT3); each perturbation prolongs action potential duration and, during slow pacing, generates early afterdepolarizations, the cellular substrate for torsades de pointes.
nodes.id: O'Hara-Rudy (ORd) Human Ventricular Action Potential Model

evidence.reference_title: Simulation of the undiseased human cardiac ventricular action potential: model formulation and experimental validation.
evidence.snippet: an accurate model for the undiseased human ventricular action potential (AP) which reproduces a broad range of physiological behaviors is needed

nodes.description: In the SCN5A-mediated subtype, defective sodium-channel inactivation leaves a small persistent inward current during depolarization, further delaying myocardial repolarization.

evidence.reference_title: Congenital long-QT syndrome caused by a novel mutation in a conserved acidic domain of the cardiac Na+ channel.
evidence.snippet: Two-electrode voltage-clamp recordings of a recombinant E1784K mutant channel expressed in Xenopus oocytes revealed a defect in fast inactivation characterized by a small, persistent current during long membrane depolarizations.

evidence.reference_title: Congenital long-QT syndrome caused by a novel mutation in a conserved acidic domain of the cardiac Na+ channel.
evidence.snippet: The functional defect exhibited by the mutant channel causes delayed myocardial repolarization

nodes.description: Baseline QT prolongation on ECG is the defining diagnostic sign of inherited long QT syndrome, although some gene carriers may have borderline or intermittently normal QTc measurements.

evidence.reference_title: Congenital long QT syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Congenital long QT syndrome (LQTS) is a hereditary cardiac disease characterized by a prolongation of the QT interval at basal ECG and by a high risk of life-threatening arrhythmias.

nodes.description: Many inherited long-QT subtypes reduce outward delayed rectifier potassium currents in cardiomyocytes, diminishing repolarization reserve and delaying completion of the ventricular action potential.

evidence.snippet: variant p.F68C causes a severe trafficking defect and exerts a dominant-negative effect on wild-type hERG channels, leading to markedly reduced rapid delayed rectifier potassium current (IKr)

nodes.description: Loss of effective cardiac output during sustained torsades de pointes causes transient cerebral hypoperfusion (syncope) and, when the malignant ventricular arrhythmia does not terminate, sudden cardiac death. These are the shared sentinel clinical endpoints of inherited long QT syndrome.

nodes.description: Tomek-Rodriguez-O'Hara-Rudy refinement of the ORd model, recalibrated and independently validated across health, disease, and drug block, with a reformulated L-type Ca2+ current and a replaced hERG (I_Kr) current. It scales from single-cell ionic dynamics to whole-organ simulation including the electrocardiogram, and is a current reference human ventricular model for LQTS repolarization and drug-block studies.

evidence.reference_title: Development, calibration, and validation of a novel human ventricular myocyte model in health, disease, and drug block.
evidence.snippet: we present the design, development, calibration and independent validation of a human-based ventricular model (ToR-ORd) for simulations of electrophysiology and excitation-contraction coupling, from ionic to whole-organ dynamics, including the electrocardiogram

nodes.description: The signature malignant ventricular tachyarrhythmia of long QT syndrome is torsades de pointes, arising from prolonged repolarization and early afterdepolarizations.

computational_models.description: The qNet metric derived from this model classifies drug-induced torsades de pointes risk.
